name: Metec
description_short: METEC Resource Center is a non-profit organization offering free HUD-approved housing counseling, homebuyer education, employment training, and financial literacy programs to low-income individuals.
description_long:
METEC Resource Center operates as a community-based non-profit focused on economic empowerment through housing, employment, and financial services. The organization is headquartered in the Quad Cities area (309-676-3832) and serves clients seeking to build stable homeownership and financial independence. Their mission explicitly centers on 'empowering home ownership, employment & financial well-being,' positioning them as a holistic community development organization rather than a transactional service provider.

METEC offers free or low-cost services including HUD-approved housing counseling, homebuyer education through their Homebuyers Club Class, financial literacy instruction via Money Matters classes, and employment training covering interview skills and computer proficiency. They recently secured a HUD Older Adult Home Modification Program Grant, beginning to accept applications in August 2025, which expands their capacity to serve seniors with housing improvements. Applications are accessible during business hours (Monday-Thursday, 9am-3pm), suggesting direct in-person service delivery.

The organization distinguishes itself through grant-funded programming that provides tangible outcomes—client testimonials highlight first-time homeownership achievement and practical job readiness skills. Their HUD affiliation indicates compliance with federal standards for housing counseling, providing credibility for clients seeking unbiased guidance. The expansion into older adult home modifications demonstrates responsiveness to underserved populations and community needs beyond basic credit or financial counseling.

As a non-profit with limited online information (404 error on their About page), METEC appears to be a smaller regional organization prioritizing direct community engagement over digital presence. While their free-help model and grant funding are strengths, potential clients should verify current program availability and eligibility requirements through direct contact, as their website lacks detailed service descriptions, eligibility criteria, and comprehensive program calendars.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling services, indicating federal compliance and consumer protection standards
Free or low-cost programming funded by grants, eliminating financial barriers for low-income clients
Newly awarded HUD Older Adult Home Modification Program Grant expanding services to seniors
Comprehensive approach addressing employment, homeownership, and financial literacy simultaneously
Documented client success outcomes, including first-time homeownership achievements
In-person accessibility during business hours (Monday-Thursday, 9am-3pm) in Quad Cities area
Practical, job-ready skills training including interview preparation and computer literacy
cons: Limited online information and non-functional About page (404 error) suggests weak digital presence and difficulty researching services
No detailed program eligibility criteria, cost structure, or comprehensive service descriptions available on website
Geographic limitation to Quad Cities area; unclear if services are available beyond this region
Minimal calendar visibility—only 3 upcoming classes shown with no full schedule or registration details provided
No contact form or online application process visible; clients must visit in person to apply for new HUD programs
best_for: First-time homebuyers in the Quad Cities area seeking free HUD-approved counseling and education
Low-to-moderate income individuals needing concurrent employment training and financial literacy support
Older adults (55+) seeking home modifications and accessibility improvements through the newly funded HUD grant
Job seekers lacking interview skills or basic computer proficiency in underserved communities
diagnosis: METEC Resource Center is best for low-income, first-time homebuyers and older adults in the Quad Cities area seeking free, grant-funded housing and employment support with direct in-person service. The primary caveat is limited digital accessibility and lack of detailed program information online; prospective clients must contact the organization directly or visit in person to understand eligibility, current availability, and enrollment procedures.
services: Homebuyer education through Homebuyers Club classes
Financial literacy instruction via Money Matters classes
Computer skills training for job readiness
HUD Older Adult Home Modification Program services (grant-funded, applications begin August 4, 2025)
Home modification assessments and planning for older adults
In-person application assistance and enrollment support
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
